# Hypotheses by content form

Use this file when selecting the right test for a specific video format.

| ID | Area / form | Hypothesis | Primary tactic | Best metric | First test |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| H166 | Talking head | Early face plus proof inserts beats uninterrupted face-only delivery for educational videos. | face open → proof/example cuts | avg % viewed, saves, follows | A: 100% face vs B: face + proof inserts |
| H167 | Talking head | Talking-head videos retain better when the first sentence names a specific viewer problem rather than a topic category. | problem-first hook | 3-sec hold | topic hook vs problem hook |
| H168 | Talking head | Direct eye contact improves trust, but too much static framing lowers completion on practical content. | eye contact + visual resets | completion, saves | static talking head vs talking head with resets |
| H169 | Tutorial / how-to | Tutorials save better when the final state is shown before the steps. | result-first tutorial | save rate, 3-sec hold | result-first vs chronological setup |
| H170 | Tutorial / how-to | Step labels improve completion in tutorials with more than two steps. | navigation text | completion | no labels vs step labels |
| H171 | Tutorial / how-to | Tutorials with one visible mistake and one fix outperform general tip lists. | mistake → fix | completion, saves | 3 tips vs mistake/fix |
| H172 | Screen recording / software | Zoomed screen recordings retain better than full-screen recordings on mobile. | mobile-legible zooms | avg watch, saves | full UI vs zoomed UI |
| H173 | Screen recording / software | Cursor/highlight annotations increase saves when UI targets are small. | guided attention | saves, rewatch | plain screen vs annotated screen |
| H174 | Screen recording / software | Screen tutorials need a verbal or text promise in the first 2 seconds because UI alone is often visually ambiguous. | keyword overlay + hook | chose-to-view | raw screen open vs labelled screen open |
| H175 | Podcast / interview clip | Podcast clips perform better when opened with the strongest claim, not the start of the answer. | cold-open claim | 3-sec hold | chronological answer vs strongest line first |
| H176 | Podcast / interview clip | Adding context labels improves retention when the speaker is unknown. | speaker/topic lower-third | avg watch | no context vs speaker/problem label |
| H177 | Podcast / interview clip | Two-person reaction cuts increase perceived conversation energy compared with a static wide shot. | reaction cutaways | completion | wide shot vs reaction cuts |
| H178 | Street interview / vox pop | Street interviews retain better when the question is displayed before the answer. | question-first overlay | 3-sec hold | answer-first vs question-first |
| H179 | Street interview / vox pop | Surprising answers should be previewed in the first second to increase completion. | answer teaser | completion | teaser answer vs normal sequence |
| H180 | Street interview / vox pop | Rapid respondent variety improves rewatch if the question is simple and repeated visually. | repeated question label | rewatch, shares | one respondent vs montage |
| H181 | Skit / comedy | Comedy clips need the premise within the first second, not after character setup. | premise-first open | 3-sec hold | premise card vs slow setup |
| H182 | Skit / comedy | Rule-of-three structure increases completion in short skits. | setup → escalation → punchline | completion | two-beat vs three-beat skit |
| H183 | Skit / comedy | Comedy becomes more shareable when the target identity is obvious. | identity-specific premise | sends/shares | generic skit vs niche skit |
| H184 | Meme / remix / reaction | Reaction videos perform better when the added commentary changes the meaning, not just repeats the source clip. | transformative commentary | completion, follows | passive reaction vs analytic reaction |
| H185 | Meme / remix / reaction | Memes with niche labels increase sends inside that group but may narrow total reach. | in-group labelling | sends, reach | broad meme vs niche-labelled meme |
| H186 | Meme / remix / reaction | Remixes need a new payoff within 3 seconds to avoid feeling like recycled content. | early original beat | 3-sec hold, completion | source-first vs original angle first |
| H187 | Product demo / unboxing | Product demos convert better when the problem is shown before the product. | problem-solution demo | clicks, saves | product-first vs problem-first |
| H188 | Product demo / unboxing | Unboxings retain better when a test/result happens early rather than only aesthetic reveal. | test-first demo | avg watch | aesthetic open vs test open |
| H189 | Product demo / unboxing | Close-up tactile shots increase watch time for physical products compared with presenter-only explanation. | sensory proof | avg watch | talking demo vs close-up process |
| H190 | UGC ad / testimonial | UGC ads are more believable when they include a specific situation rather than generic praise. | specific use-case story | CTR, watch time | generic testimonial vs use-case testimonial |
| H191 | UGC ad / testimonial | Before/after evidence outperforms claims-only testimonials for utility products. | proof-first testimonial | conversion, saves | claim vs before/after |
| H192 | UGC ad / testimonial | Low-polish UGC beats polished brand footage when authenticity is the main value proposition. | native-feeling creative | watch time, CTR | polished vs native UGC |
| H193 | Livestream clip | Livestream clips need an edited setup line because raw stream context is usually missing. | context compression | 3-sec hold | raw clip vs context-labelled clip |
| H194 | Livestream clip | Clips from live Q&A perform better when the viewer question is shown first. | comment/question lead | avg watch, saves | answer-first vs question-first |
| H195 | Livestream clip | Live clips are more follow-worthy when they imply recurring access to expertise. | series/live promise | follow rate | clip only vs clip + next-live promise |
| H196 | Gaming clip | Gaming clips retain better when the stakes are stated before the play. | stakes overlay | completion | raw play vs stakes label |
| H197 | Gaming clip | Facecam/reaction increases shareability when emotional reaction is stronger than the gameplay itself. | reaction-forward edit | shares, comments | game-only vs reaction+game |
| H198 | Gaming clip | Tutorial gaming clips save better when they freeze-frame the exact mechanic. | pause + annotation | saves | continuous gameplay vs annotated freeze |
| H199 | Fitness / workout | Fitness clips save better when framed as a routine/checklist rather than inspiration. | saveable routine | saves | motivation montage vs 3-step routine |
| H200 | Fitness / workout | Form-correction clips retain better when the wrong form is shown first. | mistake-first demo | 3-sec hold, saves | correct-only vs wrong/right |
| H201 | Fitness / workout | Timed follow-along segments increase completion when the viewer can participate immediately. | follow-along design | completion | explanation vs timed demo |
| H202 | Food / recipe | Recipes stop scroll better when the finished dish is shown first. | result-first food shot | chose-to-view | ingredients-first vs finished dish first |
| H203 | Food / recipe | Recipe videos save better when ingredient amounts appear as persistent text. | utility text | saves | no amounts vs amounts on-screen |
| H204 | Food / recipe | Texture/sound close-ups increase retention for food videos when used before instruction. | sensory hook | avg watch | talking open vs texture open |
| H205 | Beauty / fashion | Beauty/fashion videos benefit from immediate before/after contrast rather than product intro. | before/after open | chose-to-view | product-first vs transformation-first |
| H206 | Beauty / fashion | Shade/fit videos save better when labels stay visible long enough to screenshot. | screenshotable labels | saves | quick labels vs persistent labels |
| H207 | Beauty / fashion | Outfit/beauty videos send better when the use case is socially specific. | occasion framing | sends | generic look vs 'job interview/date/night-out' look |
| H208 | News / commentary | Commentary retains better when the new information is stated before opinion. | fact-before-opinion | completion, trust | opinion-first vs fact-first |
| H209 | News / commentary | Explainer clips save better when they include a timeline or causal chain. | timeline structure | saves | rant vs timeline explainer |
| H210 | News / commentary | Hot-take commentary gets more comments but weaker trust if no source/proof is shown. | proof-backed hot take | comments + follows | hot take vs sourced hot take |
| H211 | Storytime | Storytime videos need a visible stakes line before chronology begins. | stakes-first hook | 3-sec hold | chronological open vs stakes-first |
| H212 | Storytime | Storytimes retain better when each scene has a text label showing progression. | scene labels | completion | no labels vs scene labels |
| H213 | Storytime | A strong final lesson increases saves/follows more than a pure anecdotal ending. | lesson ending | saves, follows | story-only vs takeaway ending |
| H214 | Behind-the-scenes / process | Process videos stop scroll better when the final output is previewed first. | output preview | chose-to-view | process-first vs result-first |
| H215 | Behind-the-scenes / process | BTS videos build trust when they reveal a decision, not just aesthetics. | decision commentary | follows | pretty process vs decision process |
| H216 | Behind-the-scenes / process | Time-lapse process needs milestone labels to avoid becoming visual noise. | milestone labels | completion | unlabelled time-lapse vs labelled milestones |
| H217 | Case study / teardown | Teardowns convert to follows better when they diagnose a specific mechanism rather than praise the example. | mechanism diagnosis | follows | praise video vs mechanism teardown |
| H218 | Case study / teardown | Case studies save better when they end with a repeatable rule. | rule extraction | saves | case-only vs case + rule |
| H219 | Case study / teardown | Showing the failed version first increases attention for teardowns. | failure-first | 3-sec hold | winner-first vs failure-first |
| H220 | Comparison / review | Comparison videos save better when the criteria are named before the options. | criteria-first | saves | options-first vs criteria-first |
| H221 | Comparison / review | Reviews retain better when the verdict is previewed early and explained later. | verdict-first | completion | setup-first vs verdict-first |
| H222 | Comparison / review | Side-by-side visuals outperform sequential explanations when differences are visual. | side-by-side contrast | rewatch, saves | sequential vs split-screen |
| H223 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Carousel-style videos complete better when each slide resolves one micro-question. | micro-question slides | completion | topic slides vs question slides |
| H224 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Motion graphics need restrained animation; too much movement competes with reading. | animation restraint | completion | heavy kinetic vs restrained motion |
| H225 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Save rate rises when the final frame summarises the framework as a screenshotable card. | screenshotable recap | saves | normal ending vs summary card |
